compiled_state.title: record-test-status: stale RED marker removal -> gate still reports failed until --restart
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
After removing a RED zone marker from .test-index and staging the change (git add .test-index), record-test-status.sh still reports the test as failed due to cached status. The --restart flag is required to recover, which is not part of the standard marker-removal workflow.

## Expected Behavior
When a RED zone marker is removed and .test-index is staged, record-test-status.sh should detect the marker is gone and allow the test to pass without requiring --restart.

## Actual Behavior
record-test-status.sh reads cached test-gate-status from a prior run that saw the marker. Even though .test-index no longer contains the marker, the cached status says 'failed' and blocks the commit. Running --restart clears the cache and unblocks.

## Reproduction
1. Add a RED zone marker [test_foo] to .test-index
2. Run record-test-status.sh — status recorded as failed (tolerated)
3. Remove the marker from .test-index and git add .test-index
4. Run record-test-status.sh again — still reports failed (stale-red-marker:test_foo)
5. Run record-test-status.sh --restart — clears cache, now passes

## Observed
baff-7163 session: removing [test_partial_init_resume] marker required --restart to clear stale status.

## Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
